response.justification: The candidate demonstrates strong technical leadership, software craftsmanship, and architectural thinking across multiple domains. However, they fall significantly short on stated minimum qualifications: approximately 1-2 years of C++ experience versus the required 8 years, and large-scale infrastructure experience primarily in web services rather than compute/storage/hardware systems. While clearly talented, the explicit gaps in required technologies and infrastructure domain expertise make this unsuitable without substantial reskilling.
strengths: Strong technical leadership through Staff roles, mentoring programs, and community education on best practices
Proven software craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, architectural thinking, and coding standards established across teams
Experience with high-scale systems (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby) and cloud tooling (AWS CDK, Docker, Kubernetes)
concerns: Only ~1-2 years of C++ experience versus the 8-year minimum requirement (primary languages are JavaScript/TypeScript and DLang)
Large-scale experience is in web services and microservices, not compute infrastructure, hardware architecture, or storage systems
No evidence of DevOps/SRE infrastructure leadership or managing production cloud compute platforms at the required scale
